permanent pasture producing Beef cattle, bull in Ireland. Additional description: 19AL treatment where Bulls are offered grass silage ad libitum plus 1.5kg dry matter DM per head daily over the Winter period, turned out to pasture for 100 days and finished indoors on a concentrate ad libitum diet for 100 days and slaughtered at 19 months of age (this is the 2nd pasture phase). Additional Context: For the given production system, the appropriate allocation method is "biophysical" or "economic" since system expansion is not necessary. 

Biophysical allocation is not necessary because the system is a closed system where the inputs and outputs are well-defined, and the energy required to produce the beef is already accounted for in the production process. 

Economic allocation is the most suitable choice as it is a practical approach that takes into account the market value of the products and is consistent in application.

animal housing producing Milk, cow, raw (FPCM) in Italy. Additional description: Permanent Meadows Farm - representative of farms located in the Po plain where the cattle were fed principally with forage, compound feed and small quantities of alfalfa and corn feed, without silage feed and protein ingredients. In this system, the forage has spontaneously grown solely in the grassland of the farm and, during warmer seasons, has been left to dry into hay. Therefore, the milk produced is commonly known as “hay milk”, destined to produce Grana Padano cheese as well. Differentiating from the other plain systems, in PMF, meadows were never alternated with other crops, and there was no ploughing or harrowing, only mowing and irrigation. The cattle had the possibility to go in the outdoor paddock during the summer season.. Additional Context: For the given production system, the appropriate allocation method is:

System expansion is not necessary, as the system is already representative of a specific agricultural production system (permanent meadows farm). 

Therefore, the answer is: "none required".

Additional Context: For the permanent pasture producing Beef cattle in Brazil production system, the appropriate allocation method is biophysical.
